programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop backward crossovers, forward edges and skill combinations. programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop with Recital
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet with Recital
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Dance: Tap with Recital
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of tap dancing such as shuffle, roll, ball tap and brush to help develop and demonstrate a tap routine in a friendly and enjoyable environment.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 5
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop backward crossovers, forward edges and skill combinations. programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop with Recital
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet with Recital
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Dance: Tap with Recital
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of tap dancing such as shuffle, roll, ball tap and brush to help develop and demonstrate a tap routine in a friendly and enjoyable environment.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet with Recital
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of tap dancing such as shuffle, roll, ball tap and brush to help develop and demonstrate a tap routine in a friendly and enjoyable environment.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet with Recital
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drama
programs.description: Here's an early introduction to the stage and performance art. Through dramatic play and skits learn to express emotion with your body, face and voice, to improve your performing arts skills.

programs.name: Dance: Tap with Recital
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of tap dancing such as shuffle, roll, ball tap and brush to help develop and demonstrate a tap routine in a friendly and enjoyable environment.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun activities and games, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 5
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop backward crossovers, forward edges and skill combinations. Learn parallel stopping, backward edges and turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 4 or able to demonstrate crossovers, backward stopping, one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet, hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard. A helmet with a full visor or cage is recommended.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate -Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, and volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Cardio High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise? With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion, Cross & Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This is an intensive program designed for strong swimmers. The Bronze Medallion certification teaches how to manage complex water rescues through the development of physical fitness and judgment skills. The Bronze Cross certification teaches the difference between lifesaving and lifeguarding, the principles of emergency procedures and teamwork. The Standard First Aid (CPR C) certification provides comprehensive first aid training that covers spinal injuries, heat or cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and automated external defibrillator usage. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Star and Basic First Aid (CPR A)
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 5
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop backward crossovers, forward edges and skill combinations. Learn parallel stopping, backward edges and turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 4 or able to demonstrate crossovers, backward stopping, one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet, hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard. A helmet with a full visor or cage is recommended.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate -Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate -Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, and volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Cardio High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise? With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion, Cross & Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This is an intensive program designed for strong swimmers. The Bronze Medallion certification teaches how to manage complex water rescues through the development of physical fitness and judgment skills. The Bronze Cross certification teaches the difference between lifesaving and lifeguarding, the principles of emergency procedures and teamwork. The Standard First Aid (CPR C) certification provides comprehensive first aid training that covers spinal injuries, heat or cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and automated external defibrillator usage. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Star and Basic First Aid (CPR A)
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.

Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 5
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop backward crossovers, forward edges and skill combinations. Learn parallel stopping, backward edges and turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 4 or able to demonstrate crossovers, backward stopping, one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, and volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Cardio High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise? With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion, Cross & Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This is an intensive program designed for strong swimmers. The Bronze Medallion certification teaches how to manage complex water rescues through the development of physical fitness and judgment skills. The Bronze Cross certification teaches the difference between lifesaving and lifeguarding, the principles of emergency procedures and teamwork. The Standard First Aid (CPR C) certification provides comprehensive first aid training that covers spinal injuries, heat or cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and automated external defibrillator usage. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Star and Basic First Aid (CPR A)
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training. Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Volleyball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In volleyball, a team sport where the objective is to hit the ball so it makes contact with the opposing teams court, you will learn basics like serving, volleying, bumping, spiking and passing as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Young Artist
programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine.

programs.name: Young Artist with Caregiver
programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Badminton: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In badminton, a racquet sport that focuses on hand-eye coordination and has you using your racquet to rally for points with a ‘birdie’ you will learn basics like grip, serve and swing, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Cartooning
programs.description: Learn the various styles of cartooning. As you practice and perfect basic drawing skills you will move into illustrating scenes, demonstrating motion and creating characters with expressive faces that you can incorporate into single-box and multi-boxes cartoons.

programs.name: Club: Chess
programs.description: Are you ready to make the next move? In this activity, you will play chess in a round-robin format to improve your game through practice and play. This activity is facilitated by staff, there is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Dance: Basics
programs.description: Combining influences of lyrical, jazz, ballet, hip hop and modern dance this activity is perfect for beginners. Learn basic positions, body awareness and proper technique through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Young Artist
programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Art Club
programs.description: Experience working in a wide variety of art genres. Learn the basics of drawing, painting and sculpting, and with the help of both instruction and your imagination, make unique art projects.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Learn To Weight Train - Small Group
programs.description: Strengthen your muscles with weight training. Perfect for beginners, you will learn fundamental techniques and a variety of exercises to help enhance your strength, mobility, flexibility and stamina safely. Class size is limited to four participants.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 4
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop crossovers and backward stopping. Learn one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 3 or able to demonstrate strong backward skating and backward stopping ability.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 5
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills.  Develop backward crossovers, forward edges and skill combinations. Learn parallel stopping, backward edges and turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 4 or able to demonstrate crossovers, backward stopping, one foot turns and backward circle thrusts.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet, hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard. A helmet with a full visor or cage is recommended.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate -Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate -Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, and volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Cardio High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise? With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Multi-Sport: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

In multi-sport, you will learn a variety of sports such as basketball, soccer, and volleyball and more, through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Cardio High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise? With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion, Cross & Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This is an intensive program designed for strong swimmers. The Bronze Medallion certification teaches how to manage complex water rescues through the development of physical fitness and judgment skills. The Bronze Cross certification teaches the difference between lifesaving and lifeguarding, the principles of emergency procedures and teamwork. The Standard First Aid (CPR C) certification provides comprehensive first aid training that covers spinal injuries, heat or cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and automated external defibrillator usage. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Star and Basic First Aid (CPR A)
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion, Cross & Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This is an intensive program designed for strong swimmers. The Bronze Medallion certification teaches how to manage complex water rescues through the development of physical fitness and judgment skills. The Bronze Cross certification teaches the difference between lifesaving and lifeguarding, the principles of emergency procedures and teamwork. The Standard First Aid (CPR C) certification provides comprehensive first aid training that covers spinal injuries, heat or cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and automated external defibrillator usage. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Star and Basic First Aid (CPR A)
programs.description: This course develops swimming proficiency, lifesaving skill and personal fitness. Candidates refine their stroke mechanics, acquire self-rescue skills, and apply fitness principles in training workouts. Bronze Star is excellent preparation for success in Bronze Medallion and provides a fun introduction to lifesaving sport.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 9 years old.

programs.name: Hockey Skills
programs.description: Come and enjoy Canada’s national pastime—hockey! This activity introduces new hockey players to basic hockey skills and the rules of the game to prepare you for a house league or recreational play. Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1, Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner - Small Group
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate with Caregiver
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. A caregiver who is at least 14 years of age and a competent skater must actively participate in this activity.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage (for the child) and a CSA-approved hockey helmet (for the caregiver). All skaters require hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 1
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Learn basics like balance and posture, falling and standing up and forward movement. As you gain confidence on the ice, you will work towards forward skating, gliding, stopping and backward skating. Prerequisite: Able to stand and walk on skates with straight ankles off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 2
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop forward gliding, stopping and backward skating. Learn forward, one foot and backward glides.Prerequisites: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or the ability to skate (with a glide) for the length of the ice without falling.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn to Skate 3
programs.description: Lace-up for lessons to improve your skating skills. Develop backwards stopping, backward gliding and edges. Learn forward crossovers, slalom skating and two foot turns.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 2 or the able to demonstrate one foot glides, stopping and backward skating.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring and wear a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.
